title: Charred Algerian Semolina-Rimacinata Tagine
description: This charred and juicy Algerian dish is a perfect date-night dinner option, featuring semolina-rimacinata and stir-fry elements.

ingredients.name: chicken breast
ingredients.notes: 1 pound boneless and skinless chicken breast, cut into 1-inch pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) for charred flavor.
In a large bowl, whisk together the chicken, semolina-rimacinata, olive oil, onion, garlic, ginger, dates, aleppo pepper, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
In a large 12-inch oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat.

Transfer the chicken mixture to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is browned and cooked through, about 8-9 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 10 minutes, or until the semolina-rimacinata is toasted and charred on the bottom.
While the skillet is in the oven, stir the apricot jam and honey in a small bowl until combined.
Remove the skillet from the oven and stir in the apricot jam mixture and fresh parsley.
Simmer the dish over medium heat for 1-2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the semolina-rimacinata is coated.
Serve the Charred Algerian Semolina-Rimacinata Tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Do not overcrowd the skillet, cooking in batches if necessary, to ensure even cooking.
For added smokiness, you can broil the dish for an extra 2-3 minutes before serving, watching closely to prevent burning.
This recipe is best served fresh, but leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 2 days or frozen for up to 2 months, reheating when needed.
Before serving, check the seasoning and add any additional salt, pepper, or spices as needed.
Do not overmix the semolina-rimacinata to prevent breaking the semolina grains.
